coaching.subject: negative oracle ambiguity for 0.70 tier

coaching.coaching: Plan how section-scoped grep oracle for CLAUDE.md will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: section-scoped grep oracle for CLAUDE.md

coaching.coaching: Test the riskiest assumption behind implicit assumption about stale prose scope first.
coaching.subject: implicit assumption about stale prose scope

coaching.coaching: Pin down acceptance criteria completeness and oracle coverage with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: acceptance criteria completeness and oracle coverage

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC2's negative oracle to mechanically exclude the permitted 0.70 tier: e.g. use a pattern that matches 0.70 co-occurring with at least one of the calibration-2 criterion ids (F1, E2, G5, T5e, COH, G6) on the same line, such as `grep -n "0\.70" docs/plan-review-gate.md | grep -E "F1|E2|G5|T5e|COH|G6"` and assert it returns no hits.
evidence: Oracle negative: 'shows no line describing F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6 at 0.70'
AC2 positive oracle also states 'the doc states the 0.70 tier (T1/T4/T8/G1G2)' — meaning 0.70 legitimately appears elsewhere in the file
Defect type (6): prose-vs-structured assertion — grep returns raw lines requiring human interpretation of which criteria they reference
findings.finding: AC2's negative oracle (`grep -n "0\.70" docs/plan-review-gate.md`) will return hits for the 0.70 tier (T1/T4/T8/G1G2), which the plan explicitly says SHOULD remain in the doc — requiring human interpretation to decide whether the returned lines 'describe F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6 at 0.70'. This is a prose-vs-structured assertion defect: the proving command cannot mechanically pass/fail without reading context, making the oracle ambiguous for an executing agent.
scenarios: An agent runs the negative grep, sees hits for the T1/T4/T8/G1G2 0.70 tier lines, and cannot mechanically determine whether the oracle passes or fails.
The negative oracle silently passes even if a stale 0.70 line for F1 remains, if it's worded so the criterion names don't appear on the same line as 0.70.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the negative oracle with: `grep -n "0\.70" docs/plan-review-gate.md | grep -E "F1|E2|G5|T5e|COH|G6"` — this returns no output if the fix is correct, and fires only on lines that both mention 0.70 and name one of the calibration-2 criteria. This passes mechanically when the 0.70 T1/T4/T8/G1G2 tier line remains but the calibration-2 lines are fixed.

findings.finding: AC1's oracle (`grep -n 'plan-review-criteria-guide' CLAUDE.md`) does not constrain the hit to the 'Ticket template the gates enforce' section, yet the criterion requires the paragraph to appear 'BEFORE writing a plan' inside that specific section. A false-positive exists if either string appears elsewhere in CLAUDE.md (e.g. an existing reference in a different section).
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the flat grep with a section-scoped command: `awk '/Ticket template the gates enforce/,/^## /' CLAUDE.md | grep -c 'plan-review-criteria-guide'` and similarly for 'rebar explain'.

findings.finding: AC1's oracle (`grep -n "plan-review-criteria-guide" CLAUDE.md`) searches the entire file but the criterion requires the hit to be INSIDE the 'Ticket template the gates enforce' section. A grep hit outside that section would make the oracle pass while the AC is actually unmet (verify-command defect: substring false-positive / wrong fixture scope).
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the two grep oracles with section-scoped checks:
```
awk '/Ticket template the gates enforce/,/^## [A-Z]/' CLAUDE.md | grep -c "plan-review-criteria-guide"
awk '/Ticket template the gates enforce/,/^## [A-Z]/' CLAUDE.md | grep -c "rebar explain"
```
Each must return ≥ 1.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the AC-2 fix scope explicitly covers correcting lines 137-139 of docs/plan-review-gate.md, which currently classify G1G2 and T8 as advisory — contradicting their actual blocking posture (0.70) in criteria_routing.json — and add an oracle (e.g. `grep -n 'confident-but-routinely-ignored' docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns no hit, or the hit no longer includes T8/G1G2) to confirm that stale prose is removed.
evidence: docs/plan-review-gate.md line 137-139: 'Every other LLM criterion stays advisory (0.95), including the demonstrably false-positive-prone set (G1G2/T6/T5b/E5/E6/F4) and the confident-but-routinely-ignored set (T3/T10/T8)'
Plan AC-2 positive oracle only checks for 0.60/0.70/0.75 threshold lines; no oracle checks that the stale 'stays advisory' characterization of G1G2 and T8 is removed
ABSENCE: No AC oracle checks `grep -n 'advisory' docs/plan-review-gate.md` to verify the stale advisory categorization of G1G2/T8 is corrected
findings.finding: The plan's AC-2 fix scope implicitly assumes only the threshold number (0.70→corrected tiers) is stale, but lines 137-139 of docs/plan-review-gate.md also contain stale prose that categorizes G1G2 and T8 as advisory ('Every other LLM criterion stays advisory (0.95), including the demonstrably false-positive-prone set (G1G2/...) and the confident-but-routinely-ignored set (T3/T10/T8)'). Per criteria_routing.json, G1G2 is blocking at 0.70 and T8 is blocking at 0.70. After the threshold fix, this residual prose would internally contradict the newly stated 0.70 blocking tier in the same paragraph. No AC-2 oracle checks for removal or correction of this text.
scenarios: Author fixes the threshold numbers per AC-2 but leaves lines 137-139 intact; the gate doc now both states T8 is in the 0.70 blocking tier AND that T8 'stays advisory (0.95)' — a direct contradiction within the same section
A reader trying to understand T8's blocking posture sees contradictory information even after the 'fix'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC-2: 'The paragraph at line 137-139 that categorizes G1G2 and T8 as advisory is updated or removed to match their blocking posture at 0.70.' Add oracle: `grep -n 'T8' docs/plan-review-gate.md` should not show T8 in a sentence calling it advisory.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AC2 scope (or add a separate AC) to require that the gate doc's prose description of G1G2, T1, and T8 is updated from 'advisory/false-positive-prone/routinely-ignored' to reflect their actual `default_posture: blocking` at 0.70 in criteria_routing.json.
evidence: gate doc line 137: 'including the demonstrably false-positive-prone set (G1G2/T6/T5b/E5/E6/F4)'
AC2 oracle: 'grep -n "0.60" docs/plan-review-gate.md returns at least one hit' — verifies numeric thresholds only, not advisory/blocking labels
findings.finding: docs/plan-review-gate.md lines 137–139 currently describe G1G2 as 'demonstrably false-positive-prone' (advisory) and T8/T1 as 'confident-but-routinely-ignored' (advisory), but criteria_routing.json now classifies all three as `default_posture: blocking` at `block_threshold: 0.7`. The plan says the doc will 'match criteria_routing.json' but AC2's oracles only verify numeric threshold values (0.60/0.70/0.75 grep hits) and would pass even if these advisory/blocking label descriptions are left stale.
scenarios: After the plan is executed, authors reading gate doc lines 137-139 still see G1G2, T1, T8 described as advisory/FP-prone/ignored, contradicting the blocking posture they actually carry — the exact confusion the plan aims to prevent.
A reviewer verifying AC2's oracle finds the numeric thresholds corrected but the adjacent label text still stale, leaving the doc internally inconsistent.
findings.suggested_fix: In the gate doc's 'Scope (v1)' / calibration paragraph, move G1G2, T1, and T8 out of the advisory/FP-prone/ignored set and into the 0.70 blocking tier alongside T4. Add an AC2 positive oracle: `grep -n 'G1G2' docs/plan-review-gate.md` shows G1G2 in the 0.70 blocking tier, not in the advisory set.

findings.finding: docs/plan-review-gate.md line 138 also classifies G1G2 as 'demonstrably false-positive-prone' (advisory) and T8/T3/T10 as 'confident-but-routinely-ignored' (advisory), but criteria_routing.json shows G1G2 and T8 are now blocking at 0.70 — the same tier as T1/T4. The plan's AC-2 fix replaces the threshold number at line 135 but leaves this adjacent misclassification text intact, creating an internal contradiction in the post-fix doc: the corrected threshold section will say G1G2/T8 block at 0.70, while line 138 still says they're advisory.
findings.suggested_fix: Revise the passage at line 137–139 to reflect the full post-calibration blocking set: G6/COH/T5e/E2/G5/F1 at 0.60, T4/T1/T8/G1G2 at 0.70, E4 at 0.75; and remove G1G2 and T8 from the 'stays advisory' parenthetical.

evidence: 2 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: The AC #2 oracle (`grep -n "0\.70" docs/plan-review-gate.md` shows no line describing F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6 at 0.70) is a negative-only check: it proves the stale text is absent but does not confirm the correct replacement text (the three-tier breakdown with 0.60/0.70/0.75) was actually written. An implementer could delete the stale sentence and pass the oracle without adding any correct threshold documentation.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC #2: 'Oracle (positive): `grep -n "0\.60" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns at least one hit attributing the 0.60 threshold to F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6.'

findings.finding: AC2 fixes the stale 0.70 threshold for the 0.60-group criteria, but the surrounding gate-doc prose at line 133–135 will remain partially stale: the 'seven criteria' label and the single grouped sentence ('G6, COH, T5e, E2, G5, F1, T4 — are … at block_threshold: 0.70') will need to be restructured into at least two threshold groups (0.60 and 0.70), yet the plan specifies only an oracle for what to remove, not what coherent replacement text to write. The AC also introduces T1/T8/G1G2 (at 0.70 in the JSON) into the gate doc, which currently does not list them as blocking — but the plan is silent on whether the existing gate-doc prose needs expanding to cover these.

AC3 ('documents one short paragraph on remediation-mode eligibility') has no oracle/proving command, unlike every other AC in the plan. An executing agent has no deterministic in-session check to verify the paragraph was added and contains the required content.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC3: 'Oracle: `grep -n "certified PASS" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns at least one hit inside the new paragraph.'

findings.finding: AC-4 claims 'make lint passes (docs changes only; lint covers formatting of any touched files),' but the Makefile's `lint` target runs `ruff check src tests` and `ruff format --check src tests` — it covers Python source only, not Markdown files. The two files being touched (CLAUDE.md, docs/plan-review-gate.md) are Markdown and are not linted by `make lint`.
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC-4 to: '`make lint` passes (ruff Python-only; Markdown files are not covered by this gate — verify no pre-commit hook rejects the touched .md files).' or check .pre-commit-config.yaml for the actual Markdown gate.

findings.finding: AC3 has no proving command at all. It requires a paragraph to be added to docs/plan-review-gate.md but provides no oracle or grep to verify the paragraph exists and contains the required content (remediation-mode eligibility, PASS signature prerequisite, cross-reference to floor-eligibility story).
findings.suggested_fix:
Append to AC3:
```
Oracle: `grep -c "certified PASS" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns ≥ 1; `grep -c "remediation-mode eligibility" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns ≥ 1.
```

findings.finding: AC3 (remediation-mode paragraph in docs/plan-review-gate.md) has no verification oracle — the criterion cannot be objectively confirmed as met.
findings.location: AC3: 'docs/plan-review-gate.md documents (one short paragraph) that remediation-mode eligibility currently requires a prior certified PASS signature'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC3: 'Oracle: `grep -n "certified PASS" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns at least one hit inside the new paragraph.'

findings.finding: AC2's oracle only proves a negative (0.70 is absent for calibration-2 criteria) but does not prove the positive claim that the correct thresholds (0.60 for F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6; 0.75 for E4) ARE now present in the doc. The criterion says threshold statements 'match src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json' but no proving command validates the correct values exist.
findings.suggested_fix:
Extend AC2's oracle section:
```
# Positive checks
grep -nE '0\.60' docs/plan-review-gate.md | grep -E 'F1|E2|G5|T5e|COH|G6'   # must return ≥1 line
grep -nE '0\.75' docs/plan-review-gate.md | grep 'E4'                          # must return ≥1 line
```

findings.finding: AC2's oracle verifies only the ABSENCE of the old wrong threshold (0.70 for calibration-2 criteria) but does not verify PRESENCE of the correct replacement thresholds (0.60, 0.70, 0.75 per group) in the file.
findings.location: AC2 oracle: 'grep -n "0\.70" docs/plan-review-gate.md shows no line describing F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6 at 0.70'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2 oracle: '`grep -n "0\.60" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns at least one hit (covering F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6); `grep -n "0\.75" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns at least one hit (covering E4).'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
